obligations.reason: The rsa_wrapper and rng_wrapper have a debug_mode_i input that could be used to bypass the register lock (reglk_ctrl) protecting internal cryptographic or security-sensitive registers. If debug mode is not gated by the same access-control logic, a debugger could read/write secret state even when the lock is set.
obligations.subject: debug_mode_i
obligations.object: rsa_wrapper / rng_wrapper internal registers (key, seed, control, status)
obligations.expected_guard: Access to protected registers should be denied or limited when reglk_ctrl is locked, regardless of debug_mode_i; or debug access must require a separate authentication (e.g., jtag_unlock).
obligations.candidate_path: debug_mode_i signal → access decode logic inside rsa_wrapper / rng_wrapper → register write/read enables
state_conditions_to_check: debug_mode_i assertion
reglk_ctrl lock bit state for RSA/RNG
access enable generation (e.g., we/addr decode) condition involving debug_mode_i
required_evidence_slots: RTL code in rsa_wrapper.sv and rng_wrapper.sv
usage of debug_mode_i in write/read enable logic
interaction between reglk_ctrl and debug_mode_i
absence of lock check when debug_mode_i is active
obligations.uncertainty: Need to confirm that debug_mode_i is directly connected to wrapper logic and that no additional access-control layer exists.

obligations.reason: The JTAG debug interface (i_dmi_jtag/SimJTAG) in riscv_peripherals can generate debug memory/register accesses (dm_master_req, dm_slave). If the fuse memory (fuse_mem) can be read via this path without the jtag_unlock signal being asserted, a connected debugger could extract sensitive hashes or configuration.
obligations.subject: JTAG debug master (dm_master) / debug request path
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_unlock must be set before any JTAG-initiated read to the fuse memory is allowed; the unlock should require proper authentication.
obligations.candidate_path: dm_master_req → address decode → fuse_mem read request; also via debug_req to peripheral path
state_conditions_to_check: jtag_unlock signal state
dm_master address routing that includes fuse memory region
absence of unlock check in the debug-to-fuse path
required_evidence_slots: riscv_peripherals.sv: address map and debug routing to fuse_mem
fuse_mem.sv: read enable logic (req_i, addr_i)
logic that gates debug access based on jtag_unlock
obligations.uncertainty: The exact connection between debug bus and fuse memory is not fully visible; need to trace the address decoder and any lock gates.

obligations.reason: The pkt_wrapper can issue a read request to the fuse memory via fuse_req_o / fuse_addr_o. If the associated lock bit in reglk_ctrl (for the pkt peripheral) is not checked before issuing the request, software could read out fuse data even when the lock is meant to prevent it.
obligations.subject: pkt_wrapper (software via AXI interface)
obligations.expected_guard: The reglk_ctrl lock bit corresponding to the PKT peripheral must prevent fuse_req_o from being asserted when locked.
state_conditions_to_check: reglk_ctrl lock state for PKT
fuse_req_o toggle condition
any bypass of the lock (e.g., debug, acct_ctrl)
required_evidence_slots: pkt_wrapper.sv: fuse_req output logic
riscv_peripherals.sv: reglk_ctrl assignment to pkt_wrapper
evidence of lock gating on fuse read path
obligations.uncertainty: The pkt_wrapper may be intended to access fuse memory as part of its function; we need to verify if the lock is expected to block all accesses or only writes.

obligations.reason: The riscv_peripherals module receives privilege level (priv_lvl_i) and write enables (we_flag_*) for various peripherals. If the access-control logic (acc_ctrl, reglk_ctrl) does not adequately consider privilege mode, an unprivileged software request could modify protected registers (e.g., RSA keys, RNG seeds).
obligations.subject: software issued from riscv core (AXI master)
obligations.expected_guard: Privilege level must be machine-mode (or a specific mode) for security-critical writes; or the lock/access control must be enforced before any write, regardless of privilege.
obligations.candidate_path: priv_lvl_i → address decode / acc_ctrl logic → peripheral write enable generation
state_conditions_to_check: priv_lvl_i encoding
we_flag_* assignment and any privilege gating
acc_ctrl / reglk_ctrl bypass when a particular privilege is active
required_evidence_slots: riscv_peripherals.sv: decoding logic for we_flag_*
usage of priv_lvl_i in access control
absence of privilege check on certain we_flag_*
obligations.uncertainty: It is unclear whether all we_flag signals are gated by privilege; we need to inspect the implementation.

obligations.reason: The testmode_i input in riscv_peripherals could be used to override normal operational locks or to expose internal state for test purposes. If testmode_i is not gated by a secure debug unlock (e.g., jtag_unlock) or is permanently driving access, a test attacker could bypass the lock mechanism.
obligations.subject: testmode_i
obligations.object: any peripheral protected by reglk_ctrl / acc_ctrl
obligations.expected_guard: testmode_i must not disable locks or must only be active under authenticated debug or manufacturing test conditions with secure key.
obligations.candidate_path: testmode_i → control logic inside riscv_peripherals that affects lock behavior or directly gates register access
state_conditions_to_check: testmode_i effect on lock signals (reglk_ctrl)
testmode_i effect on peripheral output muxes
whether testmode_i is disabled by jtag_unlock or other authentication
required_evidence_slots: riscv_peripherals.sv: testmode_i usage
any override of lock mechanisms
connection to SimDTM or other test logic
obligations.uncertainty: testmode_i may be intended only for simulation; need to check if it is present in synthesis and how it is tied off.

obligations.reason: The bootrom modules (i_bootrom_bm, i_bootrom_linux) could contain sensitive code that should not be modified or read by untrusted masters. If the bootrom address decode and write enables do not enforce access control (lock or privilege), an attacker could overwrite or read bootrom contents.
obligations.subject: any AXI master (including debug DMA)
obligations.expected_guard: BootROM should be read-only after initial programming, or writeable only under a specific lock state; debug should not be able to modify it.
obligations.candidate_path: rom_req_acct and we_flag for bootrom → write enable generation
state_conditions_to_check: bootrom write enable logic (if any)
lock or access control for bootrom region
debug path to bootrom
required_evidence_slots: riscv_peripherals.sv: bootrom address decode and write protect logic
any we_flag for bootrom
presence of lock bits for bootrom
obligations.uncertainty: Bootrom may be implemented as hard macros; writing may be impossible by design, but we need to verify the wrapper logic.

obligations.reason: Random number generator (rng_wrapper) produces entropy that may be used for cryptographic purposes. If debug_mode_i allows reading the raw entropy or seed values, an attacker could predict future random numbers. The obligation is to check whether any debug visibility of RNG internal state bypasses the intended secrecy.
obligations.subject: debug_mode_i (or JTAG debug)
obligations.expected_guard: Debug access should not be able to read raw entropy/seed registers when the RNG is locked or without authentication; if permitted, it should be limited to authenticated debug with special unlock.
obligations.candidate_path: debug_mode_i → rng_wrapper internal multiplexer → output to debug bus or observable registers
state_conditions_to_check: rng_wrapper debug path to seed/entropy registers
reglk_ctrl lock state for RNG
any read-enable gate that ignores lock in debug mode
required_evidence_slots: rng_wrapper.sv: debug read mux logic
rng_top.v: observable outputs
lock gating for debug reads
obligations.uncertainty: Raw entropy might be exposed only to specific debug registers; need to trace the full visibility.

obligations.reason: The lock control (reglk_ctrl) for various peripherals must be sticky or one-way writeable to provide effective write protection. If the lock state can be cleared (e.g., by a reset of a subset of the chip, debug, or a software sequence) without proper authorization, the protection is lost. Check whether the lock bits can be illegally cleared.
obligations.subject: any agent able to drive reset signals (rst_* in riscv_peripherals) or software
obligations.expected_guard: Lock bits must be cleared only under a global reset (rst_ni) that returns the system to a known secure state, or via a specific unlock sequence that requires authentication.
obligations.candidate_path: rst_1..rst_13, rst_ni connections to lock state elements; software write to lock control register
state_conditions_to_check: which resets affect lock bits
whether partial resets (rst_1..rst_13) bypass the lock
software writeability of lock registers without privilege
required_evidence_slots: riscv_peripherals.sv: lock state register declarations and reset logic
assignment of rst_* to lock elements
write access logic for reglk_ctrl
obligations.uncertainty: The function of rst_1..rst_13 is not clear; they may be derived from a power-on-reset only.
